elements.text: B5-7-04, High LTV Refinance Representations and Warranties (03/01/2023)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic contains information about the representations and warranties for the high LTV refinance option, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Lender Representations and Warranties
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Applicability of the Representation and Warranty Framework
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Lender Representations and Warranties
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: For high LTV refinance loans, lenders are responsible for the standard representations and warranties described in the Selling Guide, with a number of exceptions as noted below.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For all High LTV Refinances
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: If the lender obtains an appraisal for the subject property, the lender is not responsible for the standard representations and warranties related to the marketability and condition of the property as reflected in the property valuation. The lender remains responsible for the appraisal as it pertains to the value.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Lenders may deliver loans on properties with a condition rating of C6 and/or a quality rating of Q6 completed on an 'as-is' basis. There is no requirement for the appraisal to be completed 'subject to' repairs being made.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: For properties in a project, the lender is not responsible for the standard representations and warranties related to project eligibility. However, the lender must represent and warrant the property is not a condo or co-op hotel or motel, houseboat project, or a timeshare or segmented ownership project.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Loans Underwritten in DU
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The lender is not responsible for any of the representations and warranties associated with the loan being refinanced.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: The lender is relieved of the standard underwriting representations and warranties (eligibility, credit history, liabilities, income and asset assessment) with respect to the new mortgage loan if the lender meets all of the following requirements:
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: All data in the loan casefile is complete, accurate, and not fraudulent.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The lender follows the instructions in the DU Underwriting Findings report regarding income, employment, asset, and fieldwork documentation.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The lender complies with all other requirements described in A2-2-04, Limited Waiver and Enforcement Relief of Representations and Warranties
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The lender complies with the requirements described in  A2-2-06, Representations and Warranties on Property Value.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Manually Underwritten Loans
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The lender is not responsible for any of the representations and warranties associated with the loan being refinanced other than those related to eligibility of the existing loan being refinanced.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The lender must represent and warrant that the new loan meets all the requirements of the high LTV refinance option as described in this Guide.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Applicability of the Representation and Warranty Framework
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: High LTV refinance loans are eligible for enforcement relief in accordance with the requirements of A2-3.2-02, Enforcement Relief for Breaches of Certain Representations and Warranties Related to Underwriting and Eligibility.

elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic contains information about the high LTV refinance option, including:

elements.text: Loan-Level Price Adjustments

elements.text: Whole Loan Committing and Pooling of Loans with LTV Ratios Above 105%
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Loan-Level Price Adjustments
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The LLPAs that are applicable to high LTV refinance loans are detailed in the Loan-Level Price Adjustment (LLPA) Matrix. Caps may apply based on the LTV ratio and amortization term, and excess LLPAs will be waived at delivery.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage Insurance
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The following table provides mortgage insurance requirements for high LTV refinance loans.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the loan being refinanced...
elements.label: text

elements.text: Then...
elements.label: text

elements.text: does not have mortgage insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: mortgage insurance will not be required on the new
elements.label: text

elements.text: loan.
elements.label: text

elements.text: has existing mortgage insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: the existing mortgage insurance coverage must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: continued on the new loan.
elements.label: text

elements.text: To accomplish this, the mortgage insurer will modify
elements.label: text

elements.text: the existing MI certificate and transfer it to the new
elements.label: text

elements.text: loan. Such transfer may or may not include assignment
elements.label: text

elements.text: of a new MI certificate number. Lenders should check
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the mortgage insurer for specific requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Existing loans with financed mortgage insurance are eligible for high LTV refinance loans. There should be no difference in how coverage is continued on the refinance of such loans versus existing loans that do not have financed mortgage insurance. The existing coverage can be continued on the new loan regardless of whether the financed premium on the existing loan was paid as a single premium or a split premium.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage insurance coverage must extend for the life of the new loan, or until cancellation or termination of coverage as required by law or Fannie Mae guidelines. For example, even if a 15-year loan that is 3 years old is refinanced into a 30-year loan, the mortgage insurance coverage should be extended for the full life of the new loan.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A mortgage insurance company may charge a reasonable fee to transfer the certificate. Fannie Mae allows such cost to be rolled into the UPB of the new loan as a closing cost as long as the loan will still comply with both Fannie Mae and mortgage insurer guidelines. See B7-1-02, Mortgage Insurance Coverage Requirements for additional information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan Delivery Requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: For high LTV refinance loans, lenders must provide all applicable loan delivery data elements and special feature codes.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following special feature codes (SFC) may apply:
elements.label: text

elements.text: SFC 839 may optionally be delivered for high LTV refinance loans underwritten using the standard requirements.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: SFC 840 is required for high LTV refinance loans that are underwritten using the Alternative Qualification Path.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: These codes are to be used in addition to any other special feature codes that may apply.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Income must be reported to Fannie Mae for all high LTV refinance loans at the time of loan delivery even for those transactions where there is no maximum DTI ratio. For high LTV refinance loans with payment changes less than or equal to 20%, the lender must report the stated income on the loan application (if any). If the borrower does not state any income and the lender uses the verification of reserves option as the income source, the lender must deliver the equivalent of the new monthly payment (PITIA) as the 'Monthly Income' data element (Sort ID 291).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Lenders must report gross monthly rent in the loan delivery data for all investment properties and two- to four- unit principal residence properties, regardless of whether the borrower is using rental income to qualify for the mortgage loan. See A3-4-02, Data Quality and Integrity for additional information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Whole Loan Committing and Pooling of Loans with LTV Ratios Above 105%
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Separate committing is required for high LTV refinance loans with LTV ratios above 105%. These loans may not be delivered against standard whole loan commitments. Specific 'High LTV Refi' products are available in Fannie Mae's whole loan committing application.
elements.label: text

elements.text: High LTV refinance loans with LTV ratios above 105% cannot be included in TBA-eligible MBS, but must be included in pools specifically created for loans with LTV ratios above 105%.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Furthermore, lenders may be able to deliver high LTV refinance loans with LTV ratios above 105% into the respective Fannie Majors pool specifically available for these loans. Due to the separate pool prefixes required for loans with LTV ratios above 105%, these loans may not be delivered into standard TBA-eligible Fannie Majors pools. However, MBS deliveries of loans refinanced under this new option are eligible for securitization utilizing existing Fannie Mae pool prefixes.

elements.text: Chapter B6-1, Government Insured and Guaranteed Mortgages
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: text

elements.text: This chapter describes the requirements for selling a government mortgage loan to Fannie Mae. These include the general requirements related to all government mortgage loans as well as the requirements for specific government mortgage programs.
elements.label: text

elements.text: B6-1-01, General Government Mortgage Loan Requirements (07/05/2023)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic contains general information on government mortgage loan requirements, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Overview
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Lender Eligibility
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Mortgage Term
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Age of Loans
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Lien Requirements
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Rental Property Leases
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Mortgages Permitting Open-End Advances
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Mortgage Payments
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Notice of Transfer
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: MBS Pool Parameters
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Remittance Types
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Credit Score
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Delivery of Government Mortgage Loans
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Loan Guaranty or Insurance
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Overview
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: All eligible government mortgage loans purchased or securitized by Fannie Mae must comply with the requirements of the respective government agency. Those loans must also comply with Fannie Mae requirements for government mortgage loans as specifically addressed in this Selling Guide .
elements.label: text

elements.text: Lender Eligibility
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Most government mortgage loans can only be delivered to Fannie Mae under a variance in the Lender Contract.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage Term
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The term of a government mortgage loan may not extend more than 30 years beyond the date of the first monthly payment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Age of Loans
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: All government mortgage loans must be no more than six months old measured from the first payment date to the "Purchase Ready" date (whole loan) or the MBS pool issue date (MBS loan).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Lien Requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The security instrument for a government mortgage loan must be a first lien on the borrower's real property. The security property may be subject only to liens for taxes and special assessments that are not yet due and payable and to conditions, restrictions, and encumbrances that Fannie Mae does not consider as material. The lender must provide documentation to show that the current installments of taxes and assessments (including those that may have been attached as prior liens, but are not now in arrears) have been paid or that sufficient deposits are being collected to pay them.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rental Property Leases
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: When the property that secures the mortgage is rented, the rental agreement or lease may not include any provision that could affect significantly Fannie Mae's position as mortgagee. In some jurisdictions, leases that predate the mortgage have a superior claim to the mortgage even if they have not been recorded. Normally, a tenant's rights under a pre-existing lease remain intact on the sale of the leased premises. Accordingly, if the lease is not subordinate to the mortgage, the lender must review each lease to ensure that any tenant's rights to purchase the property, and any other rights that could affect adversely the mortgagee's interest, have been waived formally by the tenant or tenants.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages Permitting Open-End Advances
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Fannie Mae will purchase or securitize a government mortgage loan that includes an open-end advance provision only if the provision gives Fannie Mae the option not to make any advances. If funds were advanced prior to delivery, the transaction is considered a modified mortgage that is not eligible for delivery. See
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: B2-1.5-02, Loan Eligibility.
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Mortgage Payments
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Because Fannie Mae will not decline delivery submissions for slight differences in payment calculations, the lender may use any widely accepted amortization table or formula. However, the monthly payment provided as part of the delivery data and the one that Fannie Mae calculates cannot differ by more than $1.00.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Notice of Transfer
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: When lenders deliver government mortgage loans to Fannie Mae for purchase or securitization, they must report the transfer of the loan in accordance with the applicable agency's requirements, if applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: MBS Pool Parameters
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Government mortgage loans (for example, FHA-insured, VA-guaranteed, HUD-guaranteed, and RD-guaranteed) that are securitized must be pooled in government-prefix MBS pools. Government mortgage loans cannot be commingled in the same pool with conventional mortgage loans.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Remittance Types
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: For all government mortgage loans, the actual/actual remittance type is required for whole loans. The scheduled/scheduled remittance type is required for all government mortgage loans delivered into MBS. (Lenders should refer to Fannie Mae's whole loan committing application for additional information regarding eligible whole loan remittance types.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Credit Score
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: All government mortgage loans are subject to a minimum representative credit score of 620. Manually underwritten government mortgage loans with nontraditional credit are exempt from this policy.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delivery of Government Mortgage Loans
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: For government mortgage loans, the lender must report all applicable data elements at delivery, including but not limited to, the Section of the Act and certain government loan-specific special feature codes. Refer to the Fannie Mae Implementation Guide for Loan Delivery Data on Fannie Mae's website for additional information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan Guaranty or Insurance
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: For Government Mortgage Loan Guaranty or Insurance, see  B7-1-05, Government Mortgage Loan Guaranty or Insurance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: B6-1-02, Eligible FHA-Insured Mortgage Loans (08/05/2020)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic contains information on eligible FHA-insured loans, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Overview
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: FHA Higher Balance Mortgage Loans
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: FHA-Insured Adjustable-Rate Mortgages
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Other Fannie Mae Policies that Pertain to FHA Loans
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Overview
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Fannie Mae may purchase or securitize single-family loans that are insured by FHA under the following Sections of Title II of the National Housing Act:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 203(b) Home Mortgages,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Section 203(h) Home Mortgages for Disaster Victims,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Section 203(k) Rehabilitation First Mortgages,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Section 234 Condominium Units (individual mortgages only), and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Section 251 Adjustable-Rate Mortgages.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The above-listed FHA loans can only be delivered to Fannie Mae under a variance in the Lender Contract. The loans must comply with all applicable FHA laws and guidelines and the lender must obtain the required FHA mortgage insurance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Note : Each Section 203(k) mortgage loan delivered to Fannie Mae must be identified with SFC 089.
elements.label: text

elements.text: FHA Higher Balance Mortgage Loans
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Lenders may deliver higher balance FHA loans to Fannie Mae for whole loan or MBS execution. Certain FHA higher balance loans must be delivered with SFC 798. Refer to  Special Feature Codes  on Fannie Mae's website for additional information about the use of this SFC.
elements.label: text

elements.text: FHA-Insured Adjustable-Rate Mortgages
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Fannie Mae will purchase or securitize the following regularly amortizing FHA-insured ARMs that are tied to the appropriate Treasury securities index:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3/1 ARM Plan 3549, 1/1/5 cap;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: 5/1 ARM Plan 3550, 1/1/5 cap;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: 5/1 ARM Plan 3640, 2/2/6 cap;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: 7/1 ARM Plan 3551, 2/2/6 cap; and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: 10/1 ARM Plan 3552, 2/2/6 cap.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Other Fannie Mae Policies that Pertain to FHA Loans
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Fannie Mae imposes the following additional policies for FHA loans:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fixed-rate FHA-insured loans that are subject to interest rate buydowns are eligible for delivery to Fannie Mae as long as the borrower is qualified at the note rate.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: FHA-insured loans that were previously included in a Ginnie Mae MBS pool but removed due to delinquency or other reasons are only eligible for sale to Fannie Mae on a negotiated basis.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: B6-1-03, Eligible VA-Guaranteed Mortgages (02/02/2022)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic contains information on eligible VA-guaranteed loans, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Eligible VA-Guaranteed Loans
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Other Fannie Mae Policies that Pertain to VA Loans
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Eligible VA-Guaranteed Loans
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Fannie Mae may purchase or securitize loans secured by one- to four-unit residential properties that are guaranteed by the VA only under Section 3710 of Title 38 for fixed-payment loans in the United States Code. These VA loans can only be delivered to Fannie Mae on a negotiated basis.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Other Fannie Mae Policies that Pertain to VA Loans
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Fannie Mae imposes the following additional policies for VA loans:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fannie Mae will purchase or securitize fixed-rate VA-guaranteed loans that are subject to interest rate buydowns as long as the borrower is qualified at the note rate.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The dollar amount of the VA guaranty must be at least equal to 25% of the original principal amount of the mortgage loan.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Fannie Mae limits the maximum loan amount for VA loans to the one-unit conforming loan limit based on the county in which the property is located. (The one-unit limit applies regardless of the number of units in the property.)
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: B6-1-04, Eligible HUD-Guaranteed Section 184 Mortgages (12/14/2022)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic contains information on HUD-guaranteed Section 184 Indian Home Loan Guarantee Program mortgages (including Section 184A Native Hawaiian Housing Loan Guarantee Program mortgage loans), including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Overview
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: HUD Section 184 Combination Construction/Permanent Mortgages
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Delivery of HUD-Guaranteed Section 184 Mortgages
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Overview
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Any approved Fannie Mae lender may deliver HUD-guaranteed Section 184 mortgages to Fannie Mae provided the lender obtains the required HUD loan guarantee.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fannie Mae will purchase or securitize HUD-guaranteed Section 184 mortgages under the following conditions:
elements.label: text

elements.text: The HUD Section 184 mortgage must comply with HUD requirements.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The loan must be secured by one- to four-unit properties located on individual lots or in a condo, cooperative, or PUD project.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: HUD Section 184 mortgages may be secured by a manufactured home if it meets HUD's requirements.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The mortgage transaction may be a purchase or a refinance transaction.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: HUD Section 184 mortgages that are subject to an interest rate buydown plan are not permitted.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: HUD Section 184 Combination Construction/Permanent Mortgages
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Lenders must adhere to HUD's requirements concerning combination construction/permanent HUD 184 mortgages. These are limited to whole loan deliveries only.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fannie Mae will not purchase a HUD-guaranteed Section 184 mortgage that includes as part of the collateral, personal property, cash, notes, an interest in securities, royalties, annuities, and any other property that is transferable and for which a present value may be determined.
elements.label: text

elements.text: However, the construction escrow account that is required when these mortgages are closed as combination construction/permanent mortgages, as well as any partially completed improvements, must be part of the collateral.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delivery of HUD-Guaranteed Section 184 Mortgages
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Lenders must report SFC 202 when selling HUD Section 184 (and 184A) loans to Fannie Mae.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 184 loans are assumable as of the note date. When selling such loans to Fannie Mae, the Assumption Indicator in the Loan Delivery application must be "True" (which means assumable).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fixed-rate RD-Guaranteed Section 502 first mortgage loans that are subject to interest rate buydowns are eligible for delivery to Fannie Mae as long as the borrower is qualified at the note rate.

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026

elements.text: Section 502 Leveraged (Blended) Loan Program
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Fannie Mae will purchase conventional first mortgage loans that are combined with a direct, low interest rate, subordinate RD Section 502 Leveraged (Blended) Loan Program under the Community Seconds program. Lenders are not required to obtain specific approval from Fannie Mae to originate these RD Section 502 Leveraged (Blended) Loan Program mortgage loans. Any Fannie Mae-approved lender that meets RD's lender eligibility criteria may deliver mortgages originated under this program.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fannie Mae will not purchase the RD-subsidized second mortgage. The first mortgage loan must satisfy the eligibility criteria applied to any standard conventional first mortgage in addition to any RD guidelines.
elements.label: text

elements.text: See  B5-5.1-02, Community Seconds Loan Eligibility, for additional information.

elements.text: Subpart B7, Insurance
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: text

elements.text: This subpart describes Fannie Mae's requirements for mortgage, title, property, flood, liability, and fidelity insurance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter B7-1, Mortgage Insurance/Loan Guaranty
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: text

elements.text: This chapter describes Fannie Mae's policies and requirements for standard conventional mortgage insurance coverage and government mortgage loan guaranty or insurance. The chapter includes requirements related to lender-purchased and borrower-financed conventional mortgage insurance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: B7-1-01, Provision of Mortgage Insurance (04/02/2025)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic contains information on mortgage insurance, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: General Requirements
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Use of Approved Forms
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: LTV Ratio Determination in New York State
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Payment of Mortgage Insurance Premiums
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Mortgage Insurability
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Prohibition of Certain Mortgage Insurance Agreements
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: General Requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Lenders must ensure that any mortgage insurance Fannie Mae requires for a mortgage loan is in place. Lenders must obtain and be able to produce evidence of any required mortgage insurance or loan guaranty.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Unless the lender has provided another charter-compliant form of credit enhancement, the lender must obtain a primary mortgage insurance policy for a conventional first mortgage loan that has an LTV ratio greater than 80% at the time it is purchased for Fannie Mae's portfolio or securitized. (For this purpose, the LTV ratio is calculated based upon the unpaid principal balance of the mortgage loan at the time it is purchased or securitized by Fannie Mae.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: For a purchase money loan, the value used in determining the LTV ratio is the lower of the sales price or the appraised value of the security property.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: For a refinance loan, the value used in originating the loan can be derived from an appraisal, AVM, or other acceptable method.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Conventional mortgages may be insured by private mortgage insurers or state or local insuring agencies that have been approved under Fannie Mae's Qualified Mortgage Insurer Approval Requirements to insure loans sold to or serviced for Fannie Mae. For a listing of the eligible conventional mortgage insurers and their associated mortgage insurance codes, see Approved Mortgage Insurers and Related Identifiers. The website is the definitive source for approved mortgage insurers.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The form of mortgage insurance policy, including any endorsements, must be acceptable to Fannie Mae.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Use of Approved Forms
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Lenders are responsible for ensuring that only Fannie Mae-approved mortgage insurance forms and related endorsements and other forms (Forms) are used in connection with individual loans sold to or securitized by Fannie Mae. These Forms provide the terms of mortgage insurance coverage on individual loans. A list of Fannie Mae-approved Forms for each insurance provider is available on Fannie Mae's website - see Approved Mortgage Insurance Forms .
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any mortgage loan sold to or securitized by Fannie Mae that requires primary mortgage insurance (or is delivered with primary mortgage insurance even though not required) and has a loan application date on or after October 1, 2014, must be insured under one of the Fannie Mae-approved Forms. If such loan is insured under any pre-existing forms or agreements between lenders and mortgage insurers, the loan is not eligible for sale to Fannie Mae, and is subject to repurchase if identified after acquisition by Fannie Mae.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any mortgage loan sold to or securitized by Fannie Mae that requires primary mortgage insurance (or is delivered with primary mortgage insurance even though not required) and has a loan application date prior to October 1, 2014, may be insured under either
elements.label: text

elements.text: one of the Fannie Mae-approved Forms; or
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: any pre-existing forms and agreements between lenders and mortgage insurers, as long as the lender first confirms with the mortgage insurer that such forms and agreements were approved by Fannie Mae for use at the time of the loan application date.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: LTV Ratio Determination in New York State
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Under a New York statute, a mortgage insurer must issue mortgage insurance based on a determination of the 'fair market value' of the property. The term 'fair market value' is not defined in the statute, but has been defined by the New York insurance regulator as being the 'appraised value.' Per the statute, for co-op properties, the issuance of mortgage insurance must be based on the 'purchase price of the ownership interest and the proprietary lease.'
elements.label: text

elements.text: As a result, the determination of value for properties in New York is different from Fannie Mae's standard definition of value that is used to calculate the LTV ratio. The following table identifies the value calculation that is to be used for mortgage loans secured by properties in New York for policies that are based on the LTV ratio.

elements.text: Payment of Mortgage Insurance Premiums
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Premium plans for mortgage insurance may be:
elements.label: text

elements.text: monthly plans - monthly premiums from accumulated escrow deposits (with no initial payment at closing),
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: annual plans - an initial payment at closing to cover the first year's premium and annual renewal premiums thereafter paid from accumulated escrow deposits,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: single-premium plans - lump-sum premium at closing to purchase life-of-the-mortgage coverage, or
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: split-premium plans - an initial payment at closing and an ongoing monthly premium from accumulated escrow deposits.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Mortgage Insurability
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Each loan a lender delivers to Fannie Mae must be insurable. A mortgage is insurable if a mortgage insurer would not decline to insure it by reason of any fraud, misrepresentation, negligence, or dishonest, criminal, or knowingly wrongful act in origination or servicing, and would not be entitled to deny a claim by reason of any of the foregoing.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Prohibition of Certain Mortgage Insurance Agreements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Fannie Mae prohibits lenders from entering into any agreement that modifies the terms of an approved mortgage insurance master policy on loans delivered to or intended for delivery to Fannie Mae. Prohibited agreements include, but are not limited to, agreements that directly or indirectly:
elements.label: text

elements.text: modify master policy provisions for settling of claims,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: limit the right of a mortgage insurer to conduct file reviews or investigate claims,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: limit the right of a mortgage insurer to rescind coverage,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: rescind or modify coverage, or
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: restrict notice to Fannie Mae of changes in coverage status.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Further, Fannie Mae prohibits loss sharing, indemnification, settlement, or similar agreements of any kind between lenders and mortgage insurance companies that affect Fannie Mae's interest in its mortgage loans or modify the terms of an approved mortgage insurance master policy on loans delivered to or intended for delivery to Fannie Mae. Traditional captive reinsurance arrangements between a mortgage insurance company and a licensed insurer or reinsurer may be permissible so long as they do not:
elements.label: text

elements.text: affect Fannie Mae's interest in its mortgage loans, or
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: modify the terms of an approved mortgage insurance master policy on loans delivered to or intended for delivery to Fannie Mae.

elements.text: B7-1-03, Lender-Purchased Mortgage Insurance (05/27/2010)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic contains information on lender-purchased mortgage insurance, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Overview
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Lender Requirements
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Overview
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Fannie Mae accepts lender-purchased mortgage insurance coverage for all loan types except adjustable-rate mortgages that can be converted to fixed-rate mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fannie Mae will consider accepting lender-purchased mortgage insurance for convertible adjustable-rate mortgages that are in MBS pools if the lender uses the 'market rate' post-conversion disposition option and assumes all interest rate risk.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Lender Requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: When providing lender-purchased mortgage insurance, the lender must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: make any and all disclosures to the borrower that are either required by law, including the Homeowners Protection Act of 1998, or are otherwise appropriate for lender-purchased mortgage insurance coverage;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: pay for the mortgage insurance coverage as a corporate obligation with an initial premium and renewal premiums for each subsequent period of coverage, which may be a month or a year. Lump-sum premium plans that provide coverage for the life-of-the-mortgage loan also are acceptable;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: increase the servicing compensation it would otherwise be required to retain for the mortgage loan (whether the mortgage loan is submitted as a whole loan or MBS pool delivery) by at least the amount of the mortgage insurance renewal premium. (This is not required for lump-sum premium plans that provide life-of-the-mortgage coverage.);
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: keep the mortgage insurance coverage in effect until the mortgage is paid in full;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: deliver the loan with SFC 019; and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: when servicing is transferred, the lender must provide the new servicer with a list of all mortgage loans with this type of insurance that are included in the portfolio that is being transferred (identifying the applicable premium rates), explaining the premium payment obligations and procedures applicable to these mortgage loans, and transferring the accruals on deposit for the payment of future renewal premiums to the new servicer (or making an appropriate adjustment to the servicing transfer settlement).
elements.label: list_item

elements.label: section_header

elements.text: B7-1-04, Financed Borrower-Purchased Mortgage Insurance (12/15/2021)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic contains information on financed borrower-purchased mortgage insurance, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Financed Mortgage Insurance Requirements
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Ineligible Transactions
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Delivery Requirements
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Prepaid Mortgage Insurance Transactions
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Financed Mortgage Insurance Requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Financed mortgage insurance transactions are defined by all of the following characteristics:
elements.label: text

elements.text: All or a portion of the borrower-purchased mortgage insurance premium (split and single-premium plans) is included in the loan amount.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The loan amount including the financed mortgage insurance premium cannot exceed the applicable maximum Fannie Mae loan limit. See B2-1.5-01, Loan Limits.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The loan purpose is purchase, construction, or limited cash-out refinance.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The loan is secured by a one-unit property that is the borrower's principal residence or second home.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The mortgage insurance coverage amount can be standard coverage (which does not require an LLPA) or minimum coverage (with a corresponding LLPA).
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The mortgage insurance coverage amount is determined based on the base (or net) LTV ratio - the LTV ratio calculated without the financed premium.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The gross LTV ratio - the LTV ratio calculated with the financed premium - is used to determine the maximum LTV ratio permitted for the transaction. The LTV ratio may never exceed the LTV ratio allowed per the Eligibility Matrix.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: If the loan is subject to any LLPAs, the LLPAs are based on the gross LTV ratio, except for minimum mortgage insurance LLPAs, which are based on the base (or net) LTV ratio.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The lender must ensure that language related to any financed mortgage insurance premium is included either directly in the applicable mortgage insurance master primary policy or in an endorsement to that policy, which language provides that the insurance benefit paid pursuant to the 'percentage option' in satisfaction of a claim be calculated as:
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: [the claim amount minus the unamortized portion of the financed mortgage insurance premium] multiplied by the applicable coverage percentage, PLUS
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the unamortized portion of the financed mortgage insurance premium.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Certain delivery requirements for financed mortgage insurance transactions must be met. See Delivery Requirements below.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Note: Fannie Mae provides two options for limited cash-out refinance transactions that include mortgage insurance in the loan amount. A 'financed mortgage insurance transaction' requires the lender to identify the upfront financed mortgage insurance amount separately and provide the required special feature code at delivery such that the base LTV can be determined. All of the above requirements must be met for the transaction to be defined as a financed mortgage insurance transaction. A 'prepaid mortgage insurance transaction' permits the lender to include the amount of the upfront mortgage insurance premium and other allowable closing costs and prepaid items in the loan amount, and not separately identify the prepaid mortgage insurance at delivery. See Prepaid Mortgage Insurance Transactions below for additional information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Ineligible Transactions
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The following mortgage loans are not eligible for delivery to Fannie Mae if they include financed borrowerpurchased mortgage insurance:
elements.label: text

elements.text: mortgage loans secured by two- to-four-unit properties,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: mortgage loans secured by investment properties, and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: cash-out refinance loans.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Note : Lender-paid mortgage insurance premiums cannot be financed into the loan amount and are therefore not considered financed mortgage insurance transactions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delivery Requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The following delivery requirements apply to financed mortgage insurance transactions:
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Financed MI Amount and MI Financed Indicator must be delivered.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The delivery file must also contain the purchase price (for purchase transactions) and appraised value (for purchase and refinance transactions) to allow for accurate calculation of the base LTV ratio.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The loan must be delivered with SFC 281.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: All other mortgage insurance-related data elements must be provided (MI Company Name, Percent of MI Coverage, Certificate Number, and MI Source).
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: For additional information, see Uniform Loan Delivery Dataset (ULDD) on Fannie Mae's website.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Prepaid Mortgage Insurance Transactions
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Fannie Mae's refinance guidelines permit borrowers to finance the payment of closing costs, prepaid items, and points in the loan amount. When the borrower includes any portion of the borrower-paid mortgage insurance premium or monthly escrows into the loan amount (with other closing costs or prepaid items), it is considered a 'prepaid mortgage insurance transaction' and not a financed mortgage insurance transaction. For a loan to be eligible for delivery to Fannie Mae with prepaid mortgage insurance, the loan must meet all the standard requirements of this Selling Guide, and the following requirements applicable to this type of loan:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: The mortgage insurance coverage amount is determined based on the LTV ratio that is calculated after the inclusion of all the closing costs, prepaid items, and points. (The concept of 'gross LTV ratio' and 'base LTV ratio' are not applicable to prepaid mortgage insurance transactions because the financed mortgage insurance amount is not identified at loan delivery.)
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The loan is not to be delivered as a financed mortgage insurance transaction - lenders should not deliver SFC 281 or the other financed mortgage insurance data elements.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The Financed MI Premium Endorsement to the mortgage insurance policy should not be obtained.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: B7-1-05, Government Mortgage Loan Guaranty or Insurance (02/23/2016)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic contains information on government mortgage loan guaranty or insurance, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Evidence of Government Guaranty or Insurance
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Inability to Obtain Guaranty or Insurance Prior to Delivery
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Lapse of Governmental Authority
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Special Feature Code for Lapse of Government Authority
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Evidence of Government Guaranty or Insurance
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Lenders must obtain the required government guaranty or government insurance. The following table lists the acceptable forms of evidence of government guaranty or insurance:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage Type
elements.label: text

elements.text: Evidence
elements.label: text

elements.text: FHA
elements.label: text

elements.text: FHA Mortgage Insurance Certificate (HUD Form
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Mortgage Type
elements.label: text

elements.text: Evidence
elements.label: text

elements.text: VA
elements.label: text

elements.text: VA Loan Guaranty Certificate
elements.label: text

elements.text: (VA Form 26-1899)
elements.label: text

elements.text: RD
elements.label: text

elements.text: RD Loan Note Guarantee
elements.label: text

elements.text: (Form RD 1980-17)
elements.label: text

elements.text: HUD Section 184
elements.label: text

elements.text: Indian Loan Guarantee Certificate (HUD Form 53039)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Inability to Obtain Guaranty or Insurance Prior to Delivery
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Evidence of the guaranty or insurance should be obtained before loan delivery, if possible. If this is not possible, the lender represents and warrants, by delivery of the loan, all of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: A complete and satisfactory mortgage guaranty or insurance application was submitted to the government agency within the required time frame, either based on an agency's prior approval of the loan application and issuance of a commitment to insure or guarantee, or subject to an agency's delegated or automatic loan approval processing, as applicable.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The mortgage insurance premiums, funding fee, or guarantee fees were paid to the government agency within the government agency's required time frame.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The government agency has the legal authority to issue the guaranty or insurance and will have such authority for long enough to issue the guaranty or insurance within a time period that is consistent with its past practice.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: After delivery of a mortgage loan, if a lender fails to obtain the guaranty or insurance in a timely manner, as determined by Fannie Mae, the lender must repurchase the mortgage and make Fannie Mae whole for any losses incurred by Fannie Mae. In addition, Fannie Mae may suspend or terminate the lender's authority to deliver the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: mortgages for which it has not already received the government mortgage guaranty or insurance,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: any government mortgage or any particular category of government mortgage, or
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: any mortgage.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The lender must notify its Fannie Mae customer account team if the government agency declines to issue the mortgage guaranty or insurance for any reason for any loan delivered to Fannie Mae.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fannie Mae may require the lender to provide periodic reports on the guaranty or insurance status for all government mortgages sold to Fannie Mae. Such reports must be provided within the requested time frame.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Lapse of Governmental Authority
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Occasionally, a government agency's guaranty or insurance authority may lapse. This occurrence is in contrast to ordinary circumstances in which there may be a delay in obtaining the government guaranty or insurance, but there is no reason to expect the government agency not to provide the guaranty or insurance within a time period that is consistent with its past practice.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Mortgages that are not yet guaranteed or insured due to a lapse of governmental authority must be delivered with the government loan identifier in accordance with Fannie Mae's usual procedures. However, for credit enhancement purposes, Fannie Mae treats such mortgages as conventional mortgages, rather than as government mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fannie Mae will accept delivery of such mortgages only if:
elements.label: text

elements.text: The government agency is continuing to accept applications and permit lenders to create direct endorsements or conditional commitments during the period of the lapse.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The delivery is for portfolio purchase rather than issuance of MBS.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The lender agrees to the repurchase requirements described below.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The lender must repurchase the mortgage and make Fannie Mae whole for any losses incurred by Fannie Mae in the following situations:
elements.label: text

elements.text: for whole loans in Fannie Mae's portfolio, if the mortgage becomes delinquent before the insurance or guaranty is issued;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the lender fails to notify Fannie Mae of its receipt of the guaranty or insurance within 60 days of when the government agency resumes issuance of the guaranty or insurance;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the lender delivers a mortgage that the government agency cannot insure or guarantee.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Special Feature Code for Lapse of Government Authority
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: When a lender delivers mortgages during a lapse in government authority, it must include the government loan identifier in accordance with Fannie Mae's usual procedures and report SFC 001 at delivery, to indicate the existence of the lender's repurchase obligation in lieu of the government guaranty or insurance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon receipt of the guaranty or insurance, the lender must contact its Fannie Mae customer account team to request removal of SFC 001. Once it is removed, Fannie Mae's record will reflect that the mortgage is government guaranteed or insured.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Provision of Title Insurance
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Each mortgage loan purchased by Fannie Mae must have a title insurance policy in place or an attorney title opinion letter that meets Fannie Mae's requirements.

elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Title Insurer Requirements
elements.label: list_item

elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Title Insurer Requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: A title insurer must be:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026

elements.text: duly authorized and licensed, as required, to issue title insurance in the state where the property is located; and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: further evaluated in accordance with the lender's procedures for title insurer approval, which may include factors such as
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: an acceptable rating from a rating agency,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: financial strength of the title insurer,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: adequate reserves,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: record related to satisfactory title claim resolution, or
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: strength of a reinsurance arrangement subject to the guidelines below.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Note: Iowa Title Guaranty is an acceptable title guarantor for properties located in the state of Iowa.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Insurer Covered by Reinsurance
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: If the lender accepts an insurer based on the strength of a reinsurer, both the primary insurer and the reinsuring company must be licensed, as required, to issue title insurance within the state where the property is located and are in good standing with that state's insurance regulator.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Both insurance carriers must execute an Assumption of Liability Endorsement (Form 858) or an equivalent endorsement that provides for 100% reinsurance of the primary insurer's policy and a 90-day written notice of termination of the reinsurance agreement. The alternative endorsement must be attached to the title insurance policy for each individual mortgage.

elements.text: Other Requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: If a mortgage is registered with MERS and is originated naming MERS as original mortgagee of record, solely as nominee for the lender named in the security instrument and the note, and the lender's successors and assigns, then the "insured mortgage" covered by the title insurance policy must be identified in the title insurance policy as the security instrument given to MERS, solely as nominee for the lender and lender's successors and assigns. However, under no circumstances may MERS be named as the insured of a title policy.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The title insurance coverage must include an environmental protection lien endorsement (ALTA 8.1 Environmental Protection Lien Endorsement or equivalent state form that provides the required coverage).
elements.label: text

elements.text: References are to the ALTA form of endorsement, but state forms may be used as described in T erms of Coverage . As an alternative to endorsements, the requisite protections may be incorporated into the policy. For loans originated prior to January 1, 2008, endorsement forms that meet Fannie Mae's requirements at the time of origination are acceptable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Title policies may not include the creditors' rights exclusion language that ALTA adopted in 1990.
elements.label: text

elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic provides specific requirements for title insurance coverage related to certain types of loan products or types of subject properties, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Condo and PUD Unit Mortgages
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Co-op Share Loans
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Mortgages Subject to Leasehold Estates

elements.text: Other Mortgages
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Condo and PUD Unit Mortgages
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The title insurance policy for a condo or PUD unit mortgage must describe all components of the unit estate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For condo units, an ALTA 4 or 4.1 endorsement or its equivalent is required. For PUD units, an ALTA 5 or 5.1 endorsement or its equivalent is required. These endorsements must be attached to each policy or incorporated in the text of the policy.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the unit owners own the common areas of the project as tenants in common, the policy for each unit mortgage must reflect that ownership. If the homeowners' association owns the common elements, areas, or facilities of the project separately (or holds them in a leasehold estate), the title insurance on those areas must insure that ownership. This title policy must show that title to the common elements, areas, or facilities is free and clear of any objectionable liens and encumbrances, including any statutory or mechanic's liens for labor or materials related to improvements on the common areas that began before the title policy was issued.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The title policy must protect Fannie Mae by insuring the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: that the mortgage is superior to any lien for unpaid common expense assessments. (In jurisdictions that give these assessments a limited priority over a first mortgage, the policy must provide assurance that those assessments have been paid through the effective date of the policy.)
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: against any impairment or loss of title of Fannie Mae's first mortgage caused by any past, present, or future violations of any covenants, conditions, or restrictions of the master deed for the project. (It must specifically insure against any loss that results from a violation that existed as of the date of the policy.)
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: that the unit does not encroach on another unit or on any of the common elements, areas, or facilities. (The policy also must insure that there is no encroachment on the unit by another unit or by any of the common elements, areas, or facilities.)
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: that the loan is secured by a unit in a condo project that has been created in compliance with the applicable enabling statutes;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: that real estate taxes are assessable and lienable only against the individual condo unit and its undivided interest in the common elements, rather than against the project as a whole; and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: that the owner of a PUD unit is a member of the homeowners' association and that the membership is transferable if the unit is sold.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Co-op Share Loans
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: When co-op shares are recognized as real property, a title policy is required. Title evidence for a co-op share loan must ensure that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: the title is generally acceptable,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the borrower has good and marketable title to the shares, and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the co-op corporation has good and marketable title to the project.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: When co-op shares are considered personal property and therefore cannot be insured under standard title policies, the title must be generally acceptable, the borrower must have title to the shares, and the co-op corporation must have good and marketable title to the project.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages Subject to Leasehold Estates
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: A mortgage that is subject to a leasehold estate must have an ALTA Endorsement 13.1.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For loans secured by properties held by a community land trust, see Title Insurance and Delivery Requirements in  B5-5.3-03, Shared Equity Transactions: Eligibility, Underwriting and Collateral Requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: B7-2-05, Title Exceptions and Impediments (07/06/2022)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic contains information on title exceptions, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Title Exceptions
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Minor Impediments to Title for Conventional Loans
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Title Impediment - Loans Secured by Properties with Unexpired Redemption Periods
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Title Exceptions
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Fannie Mae will not purchase or securitize a loan secured by property that has an unacceptable title impediment, particularly unpaid real estate taxes and survey exceptions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If surveys are not commonly required in particular jurisdictions, the lender must provide an ALTA 9 Endorsement. If it is not customary in a particular area to supply either the survey or an endorsement, the title policy must not have a survey exception.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Minor title impediments must not materially affect the marketability of the property. The lender must indemnify Fannie Mae (as described in  A2-1-03, Indemnification for Losses) for any Fannie Mae losses that can be directly attributed to the impediment(s).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Minor Impediments to Title for Conventional Loans
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Title for a property that secures a conventional loan is acceptable even though it may be subject to the following conditions, which Fannie Mae considers minor impediments:
elements.label: text

elements.text: customary public utility subsurface easements that were in place and completely covered when the loan was originated, as long as they do not extend under any buildings or other improvements;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: above-surface public utility easements that extend along one or more of the property lines for distribution purposes or along the rear property line for drainage purposes, as long as they do not extend more than 12 feet from the property lines and do not interfere with any of the buildings or improvements or with the use of the property itself;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: mutual easement agreements that establish joint driveways or party walls constructed on the security property and on an adjoining property, as long as all future owners have unlimited and unrestricted use of them;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: restrictive covenants and conditions, and cost, minimum dwelling size, or set back restrictions, as long as their violation will not result in a forfeiture or reversion of title or a lien of any kind for damages, or have an adverse effect on the fair market value of the property;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: encroachments of one foot or less on adjoining property by eaves or other overhanging projections or by driveways, as long as there is at least a ten-foot clearance between the buildings on the security property and the property line affected by the encroachment;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: encroachments on adjoining properties, as long as those encroachments consist only of hedges or removable fences;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: outstanding oil, water, or mineral rights that are customarily waived by other lenders, as long as they do not materially alter the contour of the property or impair its value or usefulness for its intended purposes;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: variations between the appraisal report and the records of possession regarding the length of the property lines, as long as the variations do not interfere with the current use of the improvements and are within an acceptable range. (For front property lines, a 2% variation is acceptable; for all other property lines, 5% is acceptable.);
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: rights of lawful parties in possession, as long as such rights do not include the right of first refusal to purchase the property. (No rights of parties in possession, including the term of a tenant's lease, may have a duration of more than two years.);
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: minor discrepancies in the description of the area, as long as the lender provides a survey and affirmative title insurance against all loss or damage resulting from the discrepancies;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: exceptions to Indian claims, as long as the lender is insured against all loss and damage from such claims.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Title Impediment - Loans Secured by Properties with Unexpired Redemption Periods
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Certain state laws provide a 'redemption period' after a foreclosure or tax sale has occurred, during which time the property may be reclaimed by the prior mortgagor or other party upon payment of all amounts owed. The length of the redemption period varies by state and does not expire automatically upon sale of the property to a new owner. Although an unexpired redemption period will generally be deemed to be an unacceptable title impediment, Fannie Mae will consider it to be acceptable provided the following requirements are met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Requirements for Loans Subject to Unexpired Redemption Periods
elements.label: text

elements.text: The property must be located in a state where it is common and customary to sell single-
elements.label: text

elements.text: family residential property during the redemption period.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loans representing the purchase of Fannie Mae-owned properties which have been
elements.label: text

elements.text: sold during the redemption period may be subject to separate negotiations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The mortgagee policy of title insurance must take specific exception to the unexpired right
elements.label: text

elements.text: of redemption but also affirmatively insure the mortgagee against all loss arising out of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: exercise of any outstanding right of redemption, without qualification.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If any party exercises a right to redeem the mortgaged property, the loan must be paid off
elements.label: text

elements.text: directly out of the redemption proceeds with no requirement for any further action or claim
elements.label: text

elements.text: for repayment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The lender must indemnify Fannie Mae (as described in A2-1-03, Indemnification for Losses)
elements.label: text

elements.text: for any losses incurred by Fannie Mae that can be directly attributed to the exercise by any
elements.label: text

elements.text: party of a right to redeem the mortgaged property, including without limitation, a loss
elements.label: text

elements.text: related to borrower default due to a dispute with the redeeming party over the terms of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: redemption.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Fannie Mae strongly encourages lenders to provide written disclosure to borrowers of properties that are subject to unexpired redemption periods if not otherwise required by law (or disclosed by the title company).
elements.label: text

elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic contains information on the following:

elements.text: Attorney Title Opinion Letter Requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Lenders are authorized to use an attorney title opinion letter in lieu of a title insurance policy if all of the conditions in the following table are satisfied.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The attorney title opinion letter must...
elements.label: text

elements.text: be addressed to the lender and all successors in interest of the lender.
elements.label: text

elements.text: provide the following statement: We [I] agree to indemnify you and your successors in
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest in the [mortgage] [deed of trust] opined hereto, to the full extent of all losses
elements.label: text

elements.text: attributable to a breach of our [my] duty to exercise reasonable care and skill in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: examination of the title and giving of this opinion.
elements.label: text

elements.text: provide the gap coverage for the period of time between the loan closing and the recordation
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: state that the title condition of the property is acceptable and the mortgage constitutes a lien
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the required priority on a fee simple estate in the property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: list other liens and state that they are subordinate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: be given by an attorney licensed to practice law in the jurisdiction where the subject property
elements.label: text

elements.text: is located. The attorney must also be insured against malpractice in rendering opinions of title
elements.label: text

elements.text: in an amount commonly prevailing in the jurisdiction, taking into account the volume of
elements.label: text

elements.text: opinions rendered by the attorney.
elements.label: text

elements.text: be commonly acceptable in lieu of title insurance by private institutional mortgage investors
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the area where the subject property is located.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: The attorney title opinion letter must...
elements.label: text

elements.text: include language relating to the coverage normally provided by ALTA Endorsement 8.1 (Environment Protection Lien). Section (a) of the endorsement insures that there are no environmental protection liens filed in the public records that have priority over the lien of the insured mortgage; Section (b) insures that there are no state statutes that provide that liens filed after the date of the policy would have priority over the lien of the insured mortgage. An attorney may include an exception for possible subsequent super liens that could take priority over the mortgage only if the subject property is located in a state whose state statutes provide for such a super lien.
elements.label: text

elements.text: include the following if the loan is an ARM:
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The law of the state in which the property securing the mortgage is located provides that (i) the lien of the mortgage will not become invalid or unenforceable resulting from provisions in the mortgage that provide for changes in the interest rate calculated pursuant to the formula provided in the mortgage, and (ii) priority of the lien of the mortgage for the UPB of the loan, together with interest as changed and other sums advanced by the noteholder in accordance with the provisions of the mortgage, will not be lost as a result of changes in the rate of interest calculated pursuant to the formula provided in the mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: include the following if the loan is secured by a unit in a PUD:
elements.label: text

elements.text: there is no present violation of any restrictive covenants that are in the PUD constituent documents and restrict the use of the land or the forfeiture or reversion of title,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: • all dues applicable to the subject property are current and not delinquent, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: • no recorded right of first refusal to purchase the land was exercised or could have been
elements.label: text

elements.text: exercised on or before the closing date of the loan and the undersigned is unaware of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: existence or the exercise of any right of first refusal on or before the closing date of the loan.
elements.label: text

elements.text: not take exception to survey matters. In addition, the opinion must not be subject to any title exceptions other than those set forth in  B7-2-05, Title Exceptions and Impediments.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Ineligible Transactions
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The following transactions are not eligible for an attorney title opinion letter:
elements.label: text

elements.text: co-op share loans;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: loans secured by a dwelling on a leasehold estate, including leasehold estates on property owned by a community land trust;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: loans secured by a manufactured home;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: HomeStyle Refresh and HomeStyle Renovation loans;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Texas Section 50(a)(6) loans; and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: loans executed using a power of attorney.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Special Feature Code
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The lender must report Special Feature Code 155 when delivering a loan that uses an attorney title opinion letter in lieu of a title insurance policy. This code is in addition to any other SFCs that may apply (see Special Feature Codes for additional information).

elements.text: Chapter B7-3, Property and Flood Insurance
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: text

elements.text: This chapter describes Fannie Mae's requirements for property and flood insurance, including those related to coverage types, amounts, and evidence. See Servicing Guide Chapter B-2, Property Insurance Requirements, Chapter B-3, Flood Insurance Requirements, and Chapter B-6, Lender-Placed insurance for additional servicer requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: B7-3-01, General Property Insurance Requirements for All Property
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic contains information on property insurance requirements for insurers, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Overview
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Property Insurer Rating Requirements
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Exceptions to Insurer Rating Requirements
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Other Exceptions to Property Insurance Requirements
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Overview
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The borrower has the right to select the insurer of their choice to provide property insurance for the subject property, provided that the insurance meets Fannie Mae's requirements. The lender or servicer must ensure that the insurer, policy, and coverage meet Fannie Mae's requirements. In some cases, Fannie Mae may require additional coverage that differs from these requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: References to "lender or servicer" include the lender or seller at origination and the servicer for the duration of loan servicing.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Property Insurer Rating Requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The property insurance policy for the property securing any first mortgage, including master policies for project developments, must be written by an insurer that meets one of the rating requirements in the following table.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rating Agency
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rating Category
elements.label: text

elements.text: AM Best Company
elements.label: text

elements.text: 'B' or better Financial Strength Rating
elements.label: text

elements.text: Demotech, Inc.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 'A' or better Insurance Financial Stability Rating
elements.label: text

elements.text: Kroll Bond Rating Agency
elements.label: text

elements.text: 'BBB' or better Insurance Financial Strength Rating
elements.label: text

elements.text: (IFSR)
elements.label: text

elements.text: S&P Global
elements.label: text

elements.text: 'BBB' or better Insurer Financial Strength Rating
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: An insurer is only required to meet the rating category requirement for one of the rating agencies, even if they are rated by multiple rating agencies.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Exceptions to Insurer Rating Requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The following are exceptions to Fannie Mae's insurer rating requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Second Mortgages - The property insurance policy for a property that secures a second mortgage does not have to be written by an insurer that meets Fannie Mae's rating requirements, unless Fannie Mae also has an interest in the first mortgage.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Mortgage Impairment (or Mortgagee Interest) Insurance - If the lender or servicer, as applicable, is covered by a mortgage impairment (or mortgagee interest) insurance policy, and the issuer meets either the AM Best Financial Strength Rating or S&P Global Insurer Financial Strength Rating, as listed in Property Insurer Rating Requirements , Fannie Mae does not require confirmation that the borrower's property insurance coverage is with an insurer that meets Fannie Mae's rating requirements. However, in such instances, the lender should advise the borrower of Fannie Mae's requirements when it originates the loan.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Reinsurance Arrangements - Policies written by an insurer that do not meet Fannie Mae's rating requirements are acceptable provided all conditions outlined in the following table are met.
elements.label: list_item

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text
